
| Agent | Role | Status |

### 📂 Repository

Your coaching hub has **85+ lines of README navigation** covering drills, defense, offense, special teams, rides/clears, and game planning. Content is well-organized across `drills/`, `defense/`, `offense/`, `plays/`, `game-plans/`, `practice-plans/`, and `rides-and-clears/`.

**Ready to work.** Just tell me what you need — I'll route it to the right agent. Examples:
- *"Create a new drill for 2-man game reps"* → Lester
- *"Write a scouting report for Conestoga"* → Omar
- *"Add a new zone offense page"* → Stringer
- *"Check all docs for formatting issues"* → Bunk

Good question. After auditing all 40+ files across every directory, here's what I see:

---

## 🔍 Gap Analysis — Harriton Lacrosse Coaching Hub

### 🔴 Major Gaps (Missing Entire Topics)

| Gap | What's Missing | Where It'd Go |
|-----|---------------|---------------|
| **Practice Plans** | Only 1 actual plan exists (transition-focus). No plans for defense days, offense days, game-week prep, preseason, or conditioning days. | `practice-plans/` |
| **Opponent Game Plans** | Scouting report is a blank template. Schedule lists 15+ opponents but zero actual scouting reports are filled in. | `game-plans/` |
| **Face-off Program** | Wing play doc exists, but no FOGO technique, clamp/plunger mechanics, or face-off drills. | `drills/` + `plays/fundamentals/` |
| **1v1 Drills** | No dedicated 1v1 dodge/defend drill — foundational for every practice. | `drills/` |
| **Conditioning & Fitness** | No conditioning, agility, or lacrosse-specific fitness content at all. | New `conditioning/` dir |
| **Settled Offensive Sets** | You have concepts (2-man, zone beaters) but no named plays or set formations with player assignments. | `offense/` or `plays/` |

### 🟡 Moderate Gaps (Partial Coverage)

| Gap | What Exists | What's Missing |
|-----|-------------|----------------|
| **Goalie Drills** | `goalie-mastery.md` covers theory | No standalone goalie drill progressions (arc work, rapid-fire saves, outlet passing) |
| **Transition Offense** | Drills for fast breaks exist | No doc on *unsettled offense philosophy* — when to push vs. settle |
| **Pick Defense** | Sliding & recovery covers rotations | No doc on defending 2-man game, switches, or pick-and-roll defense specifically |
| **Communication System** | Referenced in defensive docs | No standalone communication glossary (calls, slides, hot/fire/help terminology) |
| **Pregame / Halftime Routines** | Nothing | Warmup sequences, halftime adjustments protocol |

### 🟢 Structural / Quality Issues

| Issue | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Duplicate file** | `defense/videos-to-learn-from/individual-defense-mastery.md` appears to be a duplicate of `aidan-maguire-ssdm-breakdown.md` |
| **Season periodization** | No phasing guide (preseason → early season → mid-season → playoffs) to sequence when content gets introduced |
| **Player development tracks** | No position-specific skill progression checklists (attackman track, middie track, LSM track, goalie track) |
| **Template inconsistency** | Main `template.md` uses different emoji sections than some existing files — Bunk could audit this |

### 📊 Content Coverage by Area

```
Stick Work     ████████████████████  90%  (6 drills, solid)
Defense Theory ████████████████░░░░  80%  (deep, some gaps)
Offense Theory ██████████████░░░░░░  70%  (concepts good, no plays)
Transition     ████████████░░░░░░░░  60%  (drills exist, no philosophy)
Special Teams  ██████████░░░░░░░░░░  50%  (EMO/MDD overview, no drills)
Game Planning  ██████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  30%  (templates only, no content)
Practice Plans ████░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  20%  (1 plan out of ~40 needed)
Face-offs      ██░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░  10%  (wing play only)
Conditioning   ░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░   0%  (nothing)
```

---

**My recommendation:** The biggest bang-for-buck items are **practice plans** (you need them daily) and **a communication glossary** (every player needs the same vocabulary). Want me to have the squad start filling any of these gaps? I'd route:

- **Lester** → Practice plan library & face-off drills
- **Stringer** → Settled offensive sets & transition philosophy
- **Omar** → Scouting report for your first opponent
- **Bunk** → Duplicate cleanup & template consistency audit

Just say the word.
